evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/qiz-security.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site says the QIZ Cryptography Management Platform supports end-to-end discovery, prioritization, remediation, and governance.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official product copy describes mapping cryptographic assets, ranking risks by context and impact, and producing step-by-step remediation plans.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding announcement positions QIZ around cryptographic posture, crypto-agility, and post-quantum cryptography management after a $17M Seed.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether buyers treat post-quantum cryptography as urgent enough to fund continuous inventory and remediation before deadlines harden.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/ollama.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site gives a one-command installer and presents Ollama as a way to build with open models from local machines to cloud usage.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official page links models, docs, pricing, GitHub, download, and account surfaces, showing a developer workflow beyond a landing page.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding announcement says Ollama raised a $65M Series B led by Theory Ventures to grow its open-source AI platform.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether cloud monetization and enterprise controls can grow without weakening the simple local developer experience.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/cableteque.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site positions Cableteque as quoting software for wire harness and cable assembly manufacturers.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product page says Cableteque extracts BOMs from customer drawings, sources materials, and fills labor templates.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The pricing page describes Cableteque as a SaaS subscription priced by quoting volume and number of users.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether AI extraction remains accurate on custom parts, poor scans, and edge-case customer drawings.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/vendelux.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The rendered official site says teams can map ICP lists, match buyers to high-ROI events, and book meetings before day 1.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product surface shows event discovery, list builder, CRM integration, meetings, pricing, login, free trial, and demo paths.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The official Series B announcement says Vendelux tracks more than 250,000 global B2B events for buyer and event intelligence.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether proprietary attendee and event data stays current enough to support automated meeting booking across long-tail events.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/prime-intellect.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes a stack for training, deploying, and continuously improving AI agents across Training, Inference, Compute, and Research surfaces.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official docs expose a product entry point for developers to install Prime and start working with the platform from a command line.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The Lab announcement describes task specification, evaluation harnesses, reward signals, rollouts, adapter training, and deployment for self-improving agents.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether customer-owned agent training loops produce durable gains outside early design-partner and research-heavy deployments.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/polysense.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes AI-native machine vision and process control for food production lines that catch quality drift before waste occurs.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product navigation exposes Qualify, Intelligence, and AutoControl modules for inspection, analytics, and process adjustment.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding report says Polysense secured $10.7M to expand its food-manufacturing quality-control platform.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether the system adapts across heterogeneous food lines quickly enough for repeatable deployment economics.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/hostie.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes Hostie as a virtual concierge for restaurants that answers guest calls, texts, reservations, takeout, and event inquiries.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The pricing page exposes public restaurant plans starting at $199 per location, with phone and texting automation tiers.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding announcement says Hostie raised a $12M Series A led by Obvious Ventures to expand restaurant hospitality automation.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether voice quality and escalation logic hold up during emotionally charged or unusual guest conversations.
